title: Malouetia odorata
intro: Malouetia odorata, also known as the "Malouetia tree," is a tropical plant species native to the tropical forests of West Africa. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Malouetia odorata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Malouetia odorata is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it can be found in various herbal preparations, including teas, tonics, and capsules, which are used to treat a range of health issues.
taxonomy: Malouetia odorata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It belongs to the genus Malouetia and is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is a type of evergreen shrub or small tree that grows in the tropical rainforests. Malouetia odorata is also known as the 'Peruvian rosewood' or 'sweet olive tree'. Its classification as an Apocynaceae species places it within the family of dogbane, oleander, and milkweed plants.

Some common side effects include gastrointestinal issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications.
